===''Pokémon the Series''=== [[File:Wikstrom anime.png|thumb|250px|Wikstrom in {{aniseries|JN}}]] ====''[[M17|Diancie and the Cocoon of Destruction]]''==== Wikstrom debuted in ''[[M17|Diancie and the Cocoon of Destruction]]'' as part of the ''World of Pokémon'' intro. During his cameo, he was seen in a battle with [[Diantha]]. ===={{aniseries|JN}}==== Wikstrom made his main series debut in ''[[JN056|Searching for Chivalry!]]''. {{Ash}} and [[Goh]] traveled to [[Kalos]] to participate in a training camp hosted by Wikstrom at the [[Castle of Chivalry]]. Wikstrom had the boys traverse through a variety of obstacle courses while wearing armor and defending themselves from Wikstrom's Pokémon. Ash and Goh eventually completed all of the challenges and earned Knight Medals for both themselves and their Pokémon. He also helped Goh evolve his {{p|Scyther}} into {{TP|Goh|Scizor}} by giving it a [[Metal Coat]] and transferring it through a [[trade machine]]. In ''[[JN060|Beyond Chivalry… Aiming to be a Leek Master!]]'', [[Rinto]] revealed that he also trained at the Castle of Chivalry. During Rinto's battle with Ash, Ash used some of what he learned from Wikstrom to be able to win the battle. In ''[[JN103|The Future is Now, Thanks to Strategy!]]'', Wikstrom was briefly seen battling [[Drasna]] at the Castle of Chivalry. In ''[[JN104|Taking Two For The Team!]]'', he met Ash prior to his [[World Coronation Series]] battle against Drasna in [[Lumiose City]] and saw that Ash's {{p|Farfetch'd}} had evolved into {{AP|Sirfetch'd}} since their last meeting. He later watched the battle and congratulated Ash after he had won it. In ''[[JN130|Toying With Your Motions!]]'', Wikstrom watched Ash's match against {{an|Leon}} in the [[Masters Eight Tournament]] finals, shedding tears at witnessing Sirfetch'd's chivalrous performance. ====Character==== True to his appearance, Wikstrom is very chivalrous in his mannerisms and choice of words, though is also very kind and offers support to anyone who seeks it. He believes in upholding the standards of chivalry in all aspects of life, as he believes chivalry to be the best method to achieve greater strength. In battle, whether it be a Pokémon battle or a fight with sword props, he attacks bravely and strives to overwhelm his opponent. Wikstrom also a strong supporter of youth, opening the Castle of Chivalry to provide young Trainers with a training ground to grow stronger and wiser. Wikstrom is also prone to sudden bursts of extreme and joyous crying when witnessing something truly chivalrous, something that provides humor and concern alike to those around him. ====Pokémon==== This listing is of Wikstrom's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Scizor |mega=yes |type1=Bug |type2=Steel |img=Wikstrom Mega Scizor.png |cap=Wikstrom's Mega Scizor |epnum=M17 |epname=Diancie and the Cocoon of Destruction |vajp= |vaen= |desc=Wikstrom used his {{p|Scizor}} in a battle against [[Diantha's Gardevoir]], with both Pokémon [[Mega Evolution|Mega Evolved]]. Despite a type advantage, Mega Scizor lost the battle in a single hit. None of Scizor's moves are known.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Aegislash |type1=Steel |type2=Ghost |img=Wikstrom Aegislash.png |epnum=JN056 |epname=Searching for Chivalry! |vajp= |vaen= |desc=Wikstrom used an {{p|Aegislash}} to impede {{Ash}} and [[Goh]] during their final challenge at the [[Castle of Chivalry]]. The battle was called off once Goh managed to get the key from Wikstrom's {{p|Klefki}}, successfully completing the challenge. Aegislash known moves are {{m|Sacred Sword}} and {{m|King's Shield}}, and its [[Ability]] is {{a|Stance Change}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Probopass |type1=Rock |type2=Steel |img=Wikstrom Probopass.png |epnum=JN056 |epname=Searching for Chivalry! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Probopass}} was one of the obstacles {{Ash}} and [[Goh]] had to overcome at the [[Castle of Chivalry]]. Using magnetism, it made it difficult for Ash and Goh to move in the armor they were wearing. None of Probopass's moves are known.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Litwick |type1=Ghost |type2=Fire |img=Wikstrom Litwick.png |epnum=JN056 |epname=Searching for Chivalry! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Litwick}} provided light for the obstacles {{Ash}} and [[Goh]] encountered at the [[Castle of Chivalry]]. None of Litwick's moves are known.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Escavalier |type1=Bug |type2=Steel |img=Wikstrom Escavalier.png |epnum=JN056 |epname=Searching for Chivalry! |vajp=Noriaki Kanze |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{p|Escavalier}} was among the obstacles that [[Goh]] encountered at the [[Castle of Chivalry]]. He battled it briefly with his {{TP|Goh|Scyther}} before it retreated. In ''[[JN103|The Future is Now, Thanks to Strategy!]]'', Escavalier was used in Wikstrom's battle against [[Drasna]] and her {{p|Altaria}}. Escavalier's known moves are {{m|Fury Cutter}} and {{m|Fell Stinger}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Dedenne |type1=Electric |type2=Fairy |img=Wikstrom Dedenne.png |epnum=JN056 |epname=Searching for Chivalry! |vajp= |vaen=Alyson Leigh Rosenfeld |desc={{p|Dedenne}} was one of the Pokémon used during the second phase of Wikstrom's training. While following his {{AP|Farfetch'd}}, {{Ash}} found it and let his guard down, receiving an electric shock. Soon after, Dedenne ran off back into the castle walls. Dedenne's only known move is {{m|Nuzzle}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Honedge |type1=Steel |type2=Ghost |img=Wikstrom Honedge.png |epnum=JN056 |epname=Searching for Chivalry! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Honedge}} attempted a sneak attack against [[Ash's Farfetch'd]], but the ambush was foiled by its Trainer. None of Honedge's moves are known.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Wikstrom |pkmn=Klefki |type1=Steel |type2=Fairy |img=Wikstrom Klefki.png |epnum=JN056 |epname=Searching for Chivalry! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Klefki}} was among the obstacles that {{Ash}} and [[Goh]] encountered at the [[Castle of Chivalry]]. To pass the final challenge, Ash and Goh were tasked with taking the key Klefki was holding. By teaming up with his {{TP|Goh|Scyther}}, Goh succeeded in grabbing the key, completing the challenge. Klefki's only known move is {{m|Fairy Wind}}.}} ====Voice actors==== {{vatable | color = {{steel color}} | bordercolor = {{steel color dark}} | ja = 花輪英司 ''Eiji Hanawa'' | ar = علي سعد ''Ali Saad'' ([[JN060]])
